She came today mainly to go to the dentist. She stopped driving because parking is difficult to find and costs a lot. She started her journey from the bus stop on Claxby Road. She says the bus was early this morning. She finds the timetable poor in the afternoon. Her husband uses the car at weekends to go to the golf club. She would like a bike but worries about the noise from the traffic.

signpost.quote: In Malawi's Majete National Park, the elephant population had been wiped out by poachers who killed elephants for their ivory. But in 2003 the park was restocked and effective law enforcement introduced.
opts: Providing the historical background that created the overpopulation problem

signpost.quote: This led to a number of problems: competition for food, hunger, and elephants routinely knocking down fences around the park.
opts: Listing the consequences of overpopulation before introducing the solution

signpost.quote: So how did this translocation process work in practice?
opts: Signalling a shift from the rationale to the detailed practical description of the process

signpost.quote: The elephants translocated to Nkhotakota settled in very well and the project has generally been accepted as a huge success — and not just for the elephants.
opts: Transitioning from the process description to the outcomes and broader benefits

signpost.quote: More than two dozen elephants have been born at Nkhotakota since relocation.
opts: Providing concrete evidence of the population success before describing ecosystem and economic benefits

1.errorText: A woman calls a job agency interested in restaurant work. The agent describes Milo's Restaurants, which has good benefits including training for all staff, and a discount at their restaurants. You need to wear a white T-shirt and black trousers. If you finish after midnight on a late shift, the company pays for a bus home. The restaurant requires a high standard of customer service, and communication skills — specifically, you need a certificate in French. The first vacancy is at the Wivenhoe Street branch — they want a breakfast supervisor responsible for checking portions and the equipment maintenance procedures. The pay starts at £9.50. The second vacancy is at City Road — junior chef, managing stock, and arranging deliveries. They are closed on Sundays, with one Monday off every four weeks.

1.errorText: Dan calls Wayside Camera Club to join. His address is 52 Marrowfield Street in Peacetown. He heard about the club from a colleague, not a relative. He wants to join competitions and meet other photographers. Full membership is £30 per year and associate membership is £20 — associate members can vote in meetings. Dan chooses full membership. His first competition's theme was 'Domestic Life'. His second was themed around sunsets — and the feedback was that the composition was too crowded. His third competition was 'Animal Magic' — the photo was taken at night and the feedback was that it was too dark.

pragmatic.speaker: Young man

pragmatic.quote: I'd expected there to be a much bigger change in the number of agricultural workers in the 19th century. But the 1871 census showed that roughly 25% of the population worked on the land.
pragmatic.speaker: Young woman

pragmatic.quote: The future looks quite bleak for bank clerks — they've been in decline since ATMs were introduced, and technology will certainly make most of the jobs they do now redundant.
pragmatic.speaker: Young man

signpost.quote: Space Traffic Management is a similar concept to Air Traffic Control, but focusing on the control of satellites.
opts: Defining the new concept by analogy with a familiar system

signpost.quote: Unfortunately, at present we don't actually have a Space Traffic Management system that works.
opts: Introducing the central problem before listing its causes

signpost.quote: But in spite of this, one thing you may be surprised to learn is that you can launch a satellite and it doesn't have to send back any information.
opts: Introducing a surprising gap in the current regulatory framework

signpost.quote: And even if the operators are willing to provide information, the information isn't easy to collect.
opts: Adding a second obstacle after the willingness problem

signpost.quote: Let's consider now some of the solutions that have been suggested.
opts: Signalling a shift from problem analysis to potential solutions

1.errorText: Greg from an employment agency calls Julie Davison about a receptionist job at Fordham Medical Centre on Chastens Road. The role involves making appointments and keeping the database updated. Julie worked at a sports centre as a nurse before getting married. The agency is providing the job because the medical centre needs someone calm and confident and with good IT skills. The position starts next month, is temporary, and the contract runs to the end of October. Hours are 7:45am to 1:15pm. Parking is available but there is no bus route.

signpost.quote: He was a writer, at the height of his career in Paris and very highly regarded by his colleagues.
opts: Establishing Hugo's literary stature before introducing the political conflict that led to his exile

signpost.quote: This kind of outspoken criticism was not well liked by the rulers of France and eventually the emperor told Victor Hugo to leave Paris.
opts: Explaining the political consequence that caused exile

signpost.quote: He produced a lot while on Guernsey — including Les Misérables — and to do this he had to spend a great deal of time in the home he had there.
opts: Introducing the house as the setting for his most productive years before describing it in detail
Questioning the quality of his work

signpost.quote: He decorated and furnished each level of the house in unique and wonderful ways and many consider the inside a 'work of art'.
opts: Concluding the section on his exile
Introducing the interior's artistic significance before contextualising its present use

signpost.quote: Today it's a museum that attracts 200,000 visitors a year.
opts: Providing a quantified measure of the house's current cultural importance
